steps.text: Let's visit the farm! Farms are home to lots of animals. Find the cow and tap it!
steps.feedback: Moo! Cows give us milk. Now find the horse!
Horses are strong — farmers use them to help with work. Now find the duck!
Quack! Ducks love splashing in water. Now find the rabbit!
Rabbits love to hop and eat vegetables! Last one — find the dog!
Dogs help farmers look after the other animals. You found them all!

steps.text: Let's go on safari! These animals live far away in hot countries. Find the lion!
steps.feedback: Roar! The lion is called the king of the jungle. Now find the elephant!
Elephants are the biggest land animals on Earth! Now find the giraffe!
Giraffes have the longest necks of any animal — to reach the tallest leaves. Now find the zebra!
Every zebra has its own pattern of stripes — just like a fingerprint. Last one — find the monkey!
Monkeys are very clever — they use their hands like we do! Safari complete!

steps.text: Everybody has feelings. Learning the words helps us talk about them. Who looks happy? Find the smiling face!
steps.feedback: A big smile! What makes you feel happy? Now find sad.
Sometimes we feel sad — that's okay, it's a normal feeling. Now find worried.
Worried means we're not sure if something will be okay. Now find angry.
Angry is a big feeling. Taking a deep breath can help. Last one — find surprised!
Surprised! Something unexpected happened. You know five important feelings!

steps.text: Welcome to the fruit shop! Fruits grow on plants and trees — they're sweet and full of goodness. Find the apple!
steps.feedback: Apples grow on trees — they can be red or green! Now find the banana!
Bananas grow in hot countries on big plants. Now find the strawberry!
Strawberries are red and grow close to the ground. Now find the orange!
Oranges are juicy and full of vitamin C. Last one — find the pear!
Pears are shaped like a teardrop. The fruit shop is sorted!

steps.text: Vroom! These are all vehicles — things we ride or drive to get from place to place. Find the bus!
steps.feedback: Buses carry lots of people at once — everyone sits together! Now find the train!
Trains travel on rails and can go very fast. Now find the tractor!
Tractors work on farms — very strong and slow. Now find the truck!
Trucks carry heavy loads from place to place. Last one — find the digger!
Diggers scoop up earth to make roads and buildings. Vroom — you found them all!

steps.text: Some fruits come from far away in hot countries. These might be new to you! Find the mango!
steps.feedback: Mangoes are orange inside and very sweet — they grow in hot countries like India. Now find the pineapple!
Pineapples are spiky outside and sweet inside. They grow in tropical places. Now find the coconut!
Coconuts grow on palm trees. Inside is coconut milk — try shaking one! Last one — find the kiwi!
Kiwis are green inside with tiny seeds. They're named after a bird from New Zealand! You've been on a fruit adventure!
